codeReview.summary: Implementation satisfies the feature scope: three rotate-keyset adaptation demo scenarios were added with correct IDs/paths/flow, canonical behavior is preserved, and CoreRoutes already contains the corresponding rotate-keyset adaptation routes. The added test covers VAL-RTK-010 by asserting scenario presence/path and rotate-keyset stepper labels when rendered through CoreRoutes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-builder skill says to invoke the agent-browser skill for manual verification, but worker transcripts commonly run agent-browser via Execute CLI directly; this can create false "procedure deviation" ambiguity even when verification is correctly done.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/ui-builder/SKILL.md:31-33 and 126-129 vs mission worker transcript line 30 (workerSessionId fe4b3741-c87c-447e-b1ef-ec7bc404b4be) showing `## Tool: Execute` calls like `agent-browser --session ...`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Architecture shared-state routing docs are stale and can mislead route-related features: they describe App.tsx as the flat route table and omit many active routes (including rotate-keyset adaptation routes), while the current app delegates to CoreRoutes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:17-27 lists only a minimal App.tsx route table; src/app/App.tsx:2,8-12 uses CoreRoutes; src/app/CoreRoutes.tsx:61-63 contains /rotate-keyset/profile|distribute|complete.

summary: PASS: d6b19ed correctly adds rotate-keyset adaptation scenarios and test coverage for VAL-RTK-010, with expected route wiring present in CoreRoutes. No feature-blocking defects found.
